content: If we map the linguistic definition of reflexion (as reflection of light off a surface to form a mirrored image) onto the LLM iterative refinement paradigm of Self-Refine, we get a novel framework where each self-feedback step in Self-Refine acts as a new reflective surface that clarifies the final output the same way multiple stacked reflections sharpen and refine a faint optical image into a clear, high-fidelity representation. This framework reframes self-refinement steps not just as incremental edits, but as successive bounces of the original 'input light' off the model's own stored memory to filter out noise and align the output to the user's implicit intent.
